message:
chore(node): update beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -672,6 +672,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2qri3","title":"Ignore multi-agent scratch artifacts cluttering git status","description":"Current workspace status is polluted by agent-local artifacts that are not product inputs: .rch-target-* directories, beads recovery DB remnants, scratch root test files, storage backup files, patch outputs, and UBS text outputs. Extend .gitignore so multi-agent sessions do not keep surfacing these transient files as untracked noise.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T02:40:03.738736329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T02:41:34.236896411Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T02:41:34.236872927Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2qyqh","title":"Fix fail-closed auth threshold in admission_budget and atomic wrapping in region_ownership","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-27T03:53:04.385559653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T03:53:04.700035204Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T03:53:04.699941760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2r9uf","title":"[fresh-eyes] Remote bulkhead request identity must fail closed on empty/duplicate handles","description":"Fresh-eyes source audit found that crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs uses request_id as the stable queued-request handle in poll_queued(), but acquire() never rejects empty or duplicate request_ids. That can make queue ownership ambiguous and can issue multiple permits for the same logical remote request. Tighten the contract so request identity is explicit and fail-closed, add focused regressions, and validate with local hygiene plus rch-only heavy checks if needed.","notes":"Source reread of `crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs` still matches the intended bead contract: empty request IDs fail closed, duplicate active/queued request IDs are rejected, active request identity is released symmetrically through `outstanding_permits`, and the direct-acquire fast path now routes behind existing queue waiters to preserve FIFO admission.

Validation status refresh on 2026-03-25:
- earlier feature-correct `rch` validation for this bead stalled near sibling `frankenengine-engine`
- fresh validation on sibling bead `bd-1zabb` has now identified a concrete current blocker on the same feature-enabled compile path: rustc fails remotely at `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/runtime_diagnostics_cli.rs:5072` with `error: prefix `false` is unknown` plus `error[E0765]: unterminated double quote string`
- local follow-up inspection shows the visible local text for that engine file currently renders clean and `git diff` for that file is empty, so the blocker appears to be sibling `franken_engine` churn / remote-sync state rather than a demonstrated `remote_bulkhead` bug in `franken_node`

Interpretation: `bd-2r9uf` remains source-complete in this repo and is currently validation-blocked by the same sibling-engine compile instability affecting other `extended-surfaces` checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RubyWolf","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:13:18.334445419Z","created_by":"DarkPuma","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:40:25.574176185Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:40:25.574084654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","bulkhead","remote"],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"DarkPuma","text":"2026-03-22 DarkPuma completed the fresh-eyes remote bulkhead request-identity pass in crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs. Root cause: request_id is the only stable queued-request handle for poll_queued(), but acquire() previously accepted empty and duplicate request_ids, which made queue ownership ambiguous and could issue multiple permits for the same logical remote request. Source fix: RemoteBulkhead now fails closed on empty request IDs (RB_ERR_INVALID_REQUEST_ID), rejects duplicate request IDs across active and queued state (RB_ERR_DUPLICATE_REQUEST), tracks outstanding permits as permit_id -> request_id so release clears active request identity symmetrically, and refuses to promote a queued request if that request_id is already active. Added focused regressions for empty request IDs, duplicate active request IDs, and duplicate queued request IDs. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs. Focused heavy validation stayed rch-only: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-node request_id -- --nocapture selected worker vmi1264463, progressed through dependency compilation, reached Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0 (/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine), and then went output-silent for >60s with no verdict. Recording this bead as source-complete and infra-limited rather than remotely green.","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:19:51Z"},{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"DarkPuma","text":"2026-03-22 DarkPuma fresh-eyes follow-up on crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs found and fixed a second-order FIFO bug in the same queue-admission path. Root cause: after a release created capacity, acquire() still issued a fresh permit immediately whenever in_flight < max_in_flight, even if older queued requests already existed. That let a brand-new request leapfrog the queue head before poll_queued() had a chance to admit the older waiter, violating the module comment about deterministic queue behavior. Source fix: queue-mode admission now respects existing waiters by refusing the direct-acquire fast path whenever queue waiters already exist, queues new arrivals behind those waiters instead, and records waiters_ahead in the queue event detail. Added focused regression fresh_acquire_cannot_leapfrog_existing_queue_waiter proving queued-1 is promoted before queued-2 after capacity opens. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs. Focused heavy validation stayed rch-only: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-node fresh_acquire_cannot_leapfrog_existing_queue_waiter -- --exact --nocapture selected worker vmi1293453, progressed through dependency compilation, reached Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0 (/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine), and then went output-silent for >60s with no verdict, reproducing the shared remote stall pattern. Bead remains source-complete and infra-limited.","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:26:44Z"},{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"BoldLake","text":"2026-03-23 BoldLake re-ran focused rch-only validation for crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs with: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 RUSTFLAGS='-Cdebuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-bd-2r9uf cargo test -p frankenengine-node remote_bulkhead -- --nocapture. Worker vmi1167313 returned exit 101 after progressing into sibling dependency compilation; rustc for /data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/lib.rs was SIGKILLed before remote_bulkhead tests executed. No new source gap surfaced in remote_bulkhead.rs beyond the already-landed request-identity and FIFO fixes, so this bead remains infra-blocked rather than source-open.","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:47:41Z"},{"id":210,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"SunnySpring","text":"2026-03-25 SunnySpring verification refresh: source reread of crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs confirms the bead logic is already present in-tree (empty request IDs fail closed, duplicate active/queued request IDs are rejected, active request identity is tracked via outstanding_permits, and the FIFO no-leapfrog regression test is present). Fresh offloaded extended-surfaces compile evidence is now green: build 29769411858530513 on worker vmi1149989 ran  and exited 0. Focused offloaded test evidence remains infrastructure-limited rather than source-failing: build 29769411858530514 on vmi1264463 ran , entered , last reported progress at 2026-03-25T19:49:12Z, kept fresh heartbeats through 2026-03-25T19:54:12Z, and reached progress_age_secs=303 / build_age_secs=407 in {

+{"id":"bd-2rb05","title":"Strengthen trace-context conformance for mixed traces and orphan spans","description":"The current TraceStore::check_conformance implementation only validates per-artifact TraceContext syntax. It does not fail a flow when artifacts mix different trace_id values or when parent_span_id references are orphaned inside the artifact set, even though the bd-1gnb contract and adjacent substrate flow expect propagated, stitchable traces without orphaned spans.

Scope:
- make conformance fail on mixed trace IDs within one artifact flow
- make conformance fail on orphaned parent references inside the checked artifact set
- add unit/integration coverage

 {"id":"bd-2yhs","title":"[10.N] Implement duplicate-implementation CI gate","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.N — Execution Normalization Contract

Task Objective:
Implement a CI gate that detects duplicate implementation semantics across tracks and blocks merges when a non-canonical track attempts to re-implement canonical logic.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Duplicate-implementation detector identifies prohibited semantic redefinitions with deterministic findings.
- CI gate blocks violating changes and emits actionable remediation guidance.
- Waiver flow is explicit, scoped, and auditable when exceptions are necessary.

Expected Artifacts:
- CI workflow + detector configuration.
- Duplicate-implementation findings report on representative fixtures.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule matching and false-positive guardrails.
- E2E tests covering pass/fail CI scenarios with fixture PRs.
- Structured violation logs with stable rule IDs.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.N] Implement duplicate-implementation CI gate", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.N] Implement duplicate-implementation CI gate" must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.N] Implement duplicate-implementation CI gate" must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.N] Implement duplicate-implementation CI gate" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.N] Implement duplicate-implementation CI gate" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:50:04.132828860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T08:17:50.478847165Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T08:17:50.478755294Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-N","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yhs","depends_on_id":"bd-zxk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ymp","title":"[11] Contract field: rollout wedge","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 11

Task Objective:
Require explicit rollout wedge description for staged enablement and safe rollback.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [11] Contract field: rollout wedge are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [11] Contract field: rollout wedge are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-2ymp/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-2ymp/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[11] Contract field: rollout wedge" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[11] Contract field: rollout wedge" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every evidence contract specifies a rollout wedge: the incremental deployment strategy with explicit percentage/stage progression.
2. Wedge must define: (a) stages (e.g., 1% -> 5% -> 25% -> 100%), (b) hold duration per stage (minimum observation window), (c) promotion criteria (metrics that must be green to advance), (d) automatic vs manual promotion decision.
3. The rollout wedge must reference the fallback trigger — if trigger fires at any stage, rollout halts and rollback executes.
4. CI rejects contracts missing rollout wedge or with fewer than 2 stages.
5. Unit test: wedge with 3+ stages, hold durations, and promotion criteria passes; wedge with single stage or no hold duration fails.
6. The wedge definition must be machine-parseable for integration with deployment automation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:32.903551969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:45:16.322035638Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:45:16.322007416Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-11","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ymp","depends_on_id":"bd-3v8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- happy-path replay still passes for ordinary offline verifier identities
- verifier SDK checker/report artifacts stay consistent with live behavior
